summary: Completed backend and frontend testing for Mazigho e-commerce application. All 19 backend API tests pass (100%). Fixed 2 frontend bugs: InteractiveOriginMap crash and TestimonialsSection not displaying content. Frontend now fully functional.

ui_bugs.issue: FIXED - Was crashing with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading fr)' when product.origin didn't have coordinates/region_name structure

integration_issues.issue: FIXED - TestimonialsSection was using wrong field names (customer_name/comment instead of user_name/content)

critical_code_review_comments: InteractiveOriginMap now has proper null checks for productOrigin.coordinates
TestimonialsSection now handles both API response formats (user_name/content and customer_name/comment)
Product detail page header renders twice - may be intentional or need cleanup in ProductDetailPageExtended.js
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend APIs tested and working. Frontend had 2 bugs that were fixed: 1) InteractiveOriginMap component crashed when product origin didn't have coordinates structure - added null checks 2) TestimonialsSection used wrong field names for content/user - updated to use correct API response fields (content instead of comment, user_name instead of customer_name). Both fixes verified working with Playwright tests.

backend_apis: GET /api/categories - Returns 6 categories with name.fr field
GET /api/products/filter/advanced - Returns 25 products with variants, labels, reviews_summary
frontend_flows: Shop page shows 25 products with filters, prices, wishlist buttons
Product detail page loads with variants and add-to-cart
Testimonials page shows testimonials with content

bugs_fixed.issue: Component tried to access productOrigin.coordinates.lat and productOrigin.region_name.fr without null checks, causing crash when origin is simple dict {fr: '...', en: '...'}
bugs_fixed.fix: Added hasMapData check and proper null guards, component now returns null if no coordinates available

bugs_fixed.issue: Used testimonial.comment and testimonial.customer_name but API returns content (multilingual dict) and user_name
bugs_fixed.fix: Updated to use testimonial.content?.[language] and testimonial.user_name with fallbacks
